segments.text: Good morning church. Today we are talking about grace in the ordinary places where we often feel unseen.
Some of us walked in tired, carrying a week that did not go according to plan. The invitation of Jesus is not to pretend we are strong, but to come honestly.
Grace does not erase the truth about our lives. Grace tells the truth and then meets us there with mercy.
When Jesus says come to me, he is not recruiting impressive people. He is welcoming burdened people into rest and into a new way of living.
That means faith becomes visible on Monday morning. It becomes patience in a meeting, forgiveness at the kitchen table, and courage to make the next right choice.
If you have been waiting until you have everything together before you respond to God, hear the good news. Today is a good day to bring him your actual life.
The church is not a room full of finished people. It is a people learning together how to receive grace and become graceful.
So this week, practice one concrete act of grace. Make the call, offer the apology, serve quietly, or pray before you react.
